item.title: Ackman's Pershing Square offers to buy Universal Music for nearly $65 billion
item.description: Bill Ackman's вҖӢPershing Square said вҖҢon Tuesday it proposed вҖӢto buy вҖӢUniversal Music Group in вҒ a cash-and-stock вҖӢdeal worth about вҖӢ55.75 billion euros ($64.31 billion).

item.title: Morning Bid: A breakthrough deal or a crude awakening?
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee:

item.title: Chinese pigs fed new menu as Beijing weans farmers off US soy
item.description: At the edge of one of the many pig farms spread across the vast, unbroken floodplains of Taizhou, a two-hour drive northwest of вҖӢShanghai, a pair of square, four-metre pools of acrid-smelling ochre liquid hold the key to cutting costly soybean use in half.

item.title: China targets Taiwan's chip prowess to evade global 'containment', Taipei government says
item.description: China is targeting Taiwan to obtain its advanced chip manufacturing вҖӢtechnology and talent as a way of breaking through international "containment" of the country, according вҖҢto a report from the island's top security agency.

item.title: Apple's foldable iPhone faces engineering snags, potential shipment delays, Nikkei Asia reports
item.description: Apple has been encountering setbacks вҖӢin the engineering test phase of вҖҢits first foldable iPhone, which could lead to delays in its mass production and product shipment вҖӢschedule, Nikkei Asia reported on Monday, вҖӢciting sources.

item.title: Stocks struggle, oil jumps as Trump's Iran deadline looms
item.description: Global stocks wavered on Tuesday, while oil prices were perched near $110 per barrel as the prospect of вҖӢescalation in the war in the Middle East and the looming deadline for a deal to be reached kept nervy investors вҖҢon the sidelines.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow
item.description: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with вҖҢits team of bankers Monday night, telling them it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at an event in June following the IPO roadshow launch, according to two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Deere settles US right-to-repair lawsuit with $99 million fund, repair commitments
item.description: U.S. agriculture equipment maker Deere on вҖӢMonday agreed to pay $99 million into a settlement fund for вҖҢfarms and farmers that are part of a class action over costs and access to repairs.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow, sources say
item.description: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with вҖӢits team of bankers Monday night, telling вҖҢthem it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at вҖӢan event in June following the IPO вҖӢroadshow launch, according to two people familiar with вҒ the matter.

item.title: Dollar kept aloft as another Trump deadline looms
item.description: The dollar stood just shy of recent highs on Tuesday as traders counted down to a U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran to open the Persian Gulf to shipping or face attacks on its вҖӢinfrastructure.

item.title: US crude oil futures rise over $1 as Trump sharpens rhetoric on Iran
item.description: U.S. crude вҖӢfutures rose more вҖҢthan $1 on Tuesday as вҖӢU.S. President вҖӢDonald Trump heightened вҒ his rhetoric вҖӢagainst Iran, вҖӢthreatening stronger action if the country вҖӢfails to вҖӢreopen the critical вҖҢStrait вҒ of Hormuz.

item.title: Samsung flags eightfold jump in Q1 profit as AI chip demand drives up prices
item.description: Samsung Electronics on Tuesday projected a record-high first-quarter profit, up more than eightfold from a year earlier and well above expectations as booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure вҖӢcaused supply bottlenecks and drove chip prices higher.

item.title: Air NZ hikes fares, cuts flights as fuel costs more than double on Iran war
item.description: Air New Zealand said on Tuesday it would вҖӢslash flights across May and June вҖҢas well as hike fares, as the Iran war disrupts global air travel and sends jet вҖӢfuel costs higher.

item.title: Broadcom signs long-term deal to develop GoogleвҖҷs custom AI chips
item.description: Broadcom said on Monday it has signed a long-term agreement with Google to develop and supply future generations of вҖӢcustom artificial intelligence chips and other components for the вҖҢcompany's next-generation AI racks through 2031.

item.title: Trump's shipping waiver does not boost oil flows within US; fuel exports soar
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's move allowing foreign-flagged вҖӢcargo ships to move fuel and other goods between domestic ports has so far had little impact on American вҖҢoil supply, according to trade data and analysts who noted that U.S. refiners and shippers are earning more profits sending fuel overseas.

item.title: OpenAI urges California, Delaware to investigate Musk's 'anti-competitive behaviorвҖҷ
item.description: OpenAI urged the California and Delaware attorneys general to consider investigating Elon Musk and his associates' "improper and anti-competitive behavior", вҖӢahead of a trial between the two sides set вҖҢto begin this month.

item.title: TRADING DAY Seeking signals from the noise
item.description: Wall Street and the Asian stock markets that were open ticked higher on Easter Monday, as investors shrugged off another rise in oil prices and more belligerent rhetoric from U.S. President Donald Trump on Iran, and awaited more concrete ceasefire news.

item.title: Phillips 66 says Q1 results hit by $900 million mark-to-market losses
item.description: Phillips вҖӢ66 said вҖҢon Monday вҖӢthat вҖӢits first-quarter вҒ results вҖӢwere hit вҖӢby nearly $900 million вҖӢin вҖӢpre-tax mark-to-market вҖҢlosses, вҒ as a sharp вҖӢrise вҖӢin вҒ commodity prices вҖӢweighed вҖӢon вҒ the вҒ refiner.

item.title: US finalizes 2.48% Medicare Advantage payment hike; insurance stocks surge
item.description: The U.S. government has finalized a 2.48% average increase вҖӢin payments to private insurers offering Medicare вҖҢAdvantage plans to older adults in 2027, a much steeper rise than the near-flat increase it proposed in вҖӢJanuary.

item.title: CBS to sell late-night hours to Byron Allen as Colbert show ends
item.description: CBS said on Monday it was turning its 11:35 p.m. ET post-local-news timeslot into a time buy, selling it вҖӢto Byron Allen, after the end of Stephen Colbert's вҖҢlate-night program in May.

item.title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
item.description: A niche acquisition by Nvidia has raised concerns among artificial-intelligence and supercomputer specialists who see the move as a test of the biggest AI chip company's commitment to maintaining вҖӢa fair playing field for chip rivals and AI data center builders.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon says it has reached deal with US Postal Service on package deliveries
item.description: Amazon.com said Monday вҖӢit has reached a new agreement with вҖҢthe U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.

item.title: War in Middle East will lead to slower growth, higher inflation, IMF chief tells Reuters
item.description: The war in the Middle East will lead to higher inflation вҖӢand slower global growth, the head of вҖҢthe International Monetary Fund told Reuters on Monday, ahead of a fresh forecast for the world economy вҖӢplanned by the global lender for next вҖӢweek.

item.title: Netflix debuts new 'Playground' gaming app for kids
item.description: Netflix doubled down on its gaming efforts on Monday, launching a new app called "Netflix Playground" that would feature games built вҖӢaround popular children's characters such as Peppa Pig and Sesame Street.

item.title: Maker of Stanley tumblers prevails in lawsuit over lead scare
item.description: A federal вҖӢjudge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers вҖҢof concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead.

item.title: Trump proposes to cut 9,400 TSA workers, $1.5 billion from budget
item.description: The White House is proposing to вҖӢcut more than 9,400 вҖҢworkers and just over $1.5 billion from the 60,000-employee Transportation вҖӢSecurity Administration that вҖӢhandles airport security operations, according вҒ to budget documents.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee, Hammack say inflation is flashing 'orange,' or worse
item.description: Cleveland Federal Reserve President Beth Hammack and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee both see inflation as a far bigger problem than employment, underscoring their support for tighter rather than looser monetary policy вҖӢas the Iran war puts upward pressure on energy prices and the job market remains вҖҢstuck in low gear.

item.title: FAA proposes to hire 2,300 air traffic controllers in budget request
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said Monday вҖӢit is proposing вҖҢto hire 2,300 air traffic controller trainees вҖӢas it works вҖӢto address a persistent вҒ lack of personnel.

item.title: White House says increased productivity means Fed can cut rates
item.description: White House economic вҖҢadviser Kevin Hassett told CNBC on Monday that he believes a "supply вҖӢshock" in the U.S. вҖӢeconomy caused by capital spending вҒ and higher productivity from artificial intelligence will вҖӢallow the Federal Reserve to вҖӢlower interest rates.

item.title: NY Fed says supply chain pressures heated up in March
item.description: Supply chain pressures heated up in March to вҖӢlevels last seen at the вҖҢstart of 2023, data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York said вҖӢMonday.
